name: Rava Dosa
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon minced green chili (serrano, Thai, or jalapeno)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine flours, farina, onion, ginger, green chili, curry leaves, yogurt, salt, and enough water to make a very thin batter. Cover and set aside for 1 hour at room temperature to rest.
2. Heat a small amount of oil over medium heat in an 8- to 10-inch nonstick griddle or frying pan until a light sprinkling of water sputters and crackles on the surface.
3. Before frying each dosa, stir the batter well. It should be thinner than crepe batter so it will spread easily on the griddle; add more water if necessary. Ladle about 1/3 cup batter onto the hot griddle in a circular motion, starting from the outside and moving in, to make a thin pancake.
4. The pancake will be very thin, and as it cooks small holes will appear throughout. Sprinkle ghee generously on top.
5. When the bottom turns golden brown, turn the pancake over and fry the other side for 30 seconds.
6. Serve folded in half, golden side out.
Note: Consistency of batter is critical to the success of dosa. If dosas come out thick and gummy, the batter needs to be thinned by adding more water. To make Masala Dosa, before removing dosas from the pan (with the golden side down), place about 3 tablespoons of Potato Masala on one half, fold over like an omelette, and slide onto a warm platter. Keep in a warm oven while frying remaining dosas. Do not stack or cover with a lid, or they will become soggy.
